channel.title: Pharma AI & Data Watch
channel.description: Curated AI, pharma, and regulatory science intelligence — updated weekly.

item.title: Enforcing all those new state-level AI laws is unlikely and thus opens the door to foul results
item.description: U.S. states are rapidly passing new AI laws. But if the enforcement is weak or nonexistent, these laws will be hollow. An AI Insider analysis and scoop.

item.title: I attended the kiro and amazon quick fiesta event in malaysia
item.description: AWS's first agentic AI event in Malaysia showcased Kiro and Amazon Quick — two products designed to turn AI agents from demos into production workflows. Here's what happened, what I learned, and why the workflow framing matters more than the speed-up promises.

item.title: A diarrhea-causing parasite is spreading. should you stop eating salad?
item.description: Salad greens could be a source of the growing cyclospora outbreak, but officials haven't identified a specific product, grower or supplier.

item.title: Join us: building secure healthcare systems
item.description: Register for our July 30 panel on reducing risk through better architecture, AI governance, and developer workflows, featuring David Pace of Merck and Joël Quenneville and Clarissa Borges of thoughtbot.

item.title: South korea making its own security-centric AI model
item.description: Adapting existing local LLM project for security and sovereignty purposes and hopes to one day match Mythos

item.title: Non-Pharmacological Interventions for Postoperative Pain in Breast Cancer Patients: A Network Meta-analysis
item.description: This network meta-analysis evaluates the effectiveness of non-pharmacological interventions for managing postoperative pain in breast cancer patients. The study synthesizes evidence from multiple treatment approaches to identify which interventions provide the most effective pain relief in this pati

item.title: Safety and efficacy of metformin for the treatment of pain: protocol for a systematic review
item.description: This protocol describes a planned systematic review to evaluate the safety and efficacy of metformin in treating pain. The review will synthesize evidence on metformin's use as a pain management intervention, with assessment of both therapeutic benefits and safety outcomes. This work addresses an em

item.title: A multicentre randomised, double-blind, double-dummy phase II clinical trial of benznidazole versus nifurtimox in adults with chronic Chagas disease in Brazil, alongside a prospective cohort: the BENBRASIL trial protocol
item.description: This protocol describes a multicentre, randomised, double-blind, double-dummy phase II clinical trial comparing benznidazole and nifurtimox in adults with chronic Chagas disease in Brazil, integrated with a prospective cohort study. The BENBRASIL trial aims to evaluate the relative efficacy and safe

item.title: FDA Approves First Oral PCSK9 Inhibitor to Lower LDL Cholesterol in Adults with High Cholesterol
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration approved Lipfendra (enlicitide), the first oral inhibitor of proprotein convertase subtilisin/kexin type 9 (PCSK9), as an adjunct to diet and exercise to reduce low-density lipoprotein cholesterol (LDL-C), or “bad” cholesterol, in adults with hypercholesterolemi

item.title: STAT+: The end for GSK’s chronic cough treatment
item.description: Sonothera's ultrasound gene therapy delivery, a new Alzheimer's drug controversy, and more biotech news from The Readout

item.title: India bets ₹10,000 crore on a leap from generic drugs to biologics
item.description: Biopharma SHAKTI aims to build the research, clinical-trial and manufacturing ecosystem India needs to move from low-cost generic copies to high-value biological medicines.

item.title: The mole in the model - you've hired an adversary | origin
item.description: A backdoor in an open-weight model survives fine-tuning and fires on a hidden trigger, so where you run it does not matter. Defense lives on the endpoint.

item.title: Scientists finally solved how a common gut bacterium triggers colon cancer
item.description: Researchers solved a long-standing mystery behind how a bacterial toxin associated with colorectal cancer damages the colon. The toxin first binds to a receptor called claudin-4, giving it access to attack the cells' protective barrier. After identifying…

item.title: Charter — AI agent memory interoperability community group (v1.0)
item.description: Group: AI Agent Memory Interoperability Community GroupGroup home: https://www.w3.org/community/ai-agent-memory-interop/Short name: ai-agent-memory-interopStatus: v1.0 — AdoptedAdopted: 2026-06-19 (call for consensus circulated 2026-06-05; carried absent sust…

item.title: Op-ed: australia vs AI copyright theft and data centres blow by blow
item.description: It’s time for AI to learn to respect property rights. The post Op-Ed: Australia vs AI copyright theft and data centres blow by blow appeared first on Digital Journal.

item.title: A global capital for AI safety is emerging — and it's not in silicon valley
item.description: The United Kingdom is gaining influence through its growing AI-safety ecosystem.

item.title: New research shows that where your sugar comes from may matter more than how much you eat
item.description: New research suggests sugar from whole fruit, dairy, and added sugars affect heart health differently. Here's what the science says about where your sugar comes from.

item.title: Health subcommittee holds hearing to examine ways to drive efficiencies across early-stage clinical development
item.description: The Committee on Energy and Commerce is the oldest standing legislative committee in the U.S. House of Representatives and is vested with the broadest jurisdiction of any congressional authorizing committee.

item.title: Former openai CTO does what altman won't: releases a frontier AI model that's actually open
item.description: Thinking Machines' first open weights model is a 975 billion parameter alternative to Chinese LLMs

item.title: Microsoft is reportedly training salespeople to talk down openai and anthropic | techcrunch
item.description: Microsoft is looking to sell its in-house AI models as more efficient and cost-effective than its competitors' models.

item.title: Applied computing wants to give oil and gas operators an AI model for the entire plant | techcrunch
item.description: Applied Computing has raised a $20M Series A to build a foundation AI model for the oil, gas and petrochemical industry.

item.title: Risk of fracture with gabapentinoid treatment in older adults: a multinational population-based study
item.description: This multinational population-based study examines the association between gabapentinoid treatment and fracture risk in older adults. The research represents a collaborative effort across multiple countries and institutions to evaluate the safety profile of gabapentinoids, commonly prescribed medica

item.title: Efficacy and safety of duvakitug in patients with Crohn's disease (RELIEVE UCCD): a phase 2b, randomised, placebo-controlled trial
item.description: 1. Lancet Gastroenterol Hepatol. 2026 Jul 16:S2468-1253(26)00119-6. doi: 10.1016/S2468-1253(26)00119-6. Online ahead of print. Efficacy and safety of duvakitug in patients with Crohn's disease (RELIEVE UCCD): a phase 2b, randomised, placebo-controlled trial. Jairath V(1), Kierkuś J(2), Duvall GA(3),

item.title: Predicting biotech clinical trials and a new Alzheimer’s drug controversy
item.description: This week on "The Readout LOUD" podcast: a new Alzheimer's drug controversy and prediction markets for clinical trials and FDA drug reviews. Listen now.

item.title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about Kalshi bets on clinical trials, a Merck cholesterol pill, and more
item.description: Kalshi, one of the world’s largest prediction market exchanges, plans to take bets on clinical trials and regulatory approvals

item.title: STAT+: FDA approves Merck’s oral PCSK9 drug, a first
item.description: Kalshi allows bets on clinical trial outcomes, Eli Lilly buys a psychedelic medicine, and more biotech news from The Readout

item.title: STAT+: Kalshi comes for biopharma, with prediction markets for clinical trials and FDA approvals
item.description: Prediction markets have permeated the worlds of sports, politics, and reality television shows. Now, the biopharma industry could be next.

item.title: STAT+: Amylyx nears pivotal endocrine drug study readout
item.description: Amylyx Pharmaceuticals will soon read out results from a study evaluating a new treatment for a rare endocrine disorder, a pivotal moment for patients and the company.

item.title: Why is OpenAI selling a ChatGPT basketball?
item.description: You may have heard that OpenAI released its first piece of hardware this week. You may not have heard about the ChatGPT basketball.

item.title: How a former DeepMind researcher raised at a $300M pre-seed valuation before launching a product
item.description: Drawing on more than a decade spent helping build some of the world's most influential AI systems, including research that later informed the development of ChatGPT, Andrew Dai explains why he believes visual AI is one of the next major frontiers in artificial intelligence.

item.title: Moonshot’s upcoming Kimi 3 is expected to close the gap with Anthropic’s Opus 4.8
item.description: The FT reports Kimi K3 will be the largest open AI model from China, with a parameter count between 2 trillion and 3 trillion.

item.title: The Download: OpenAI unveils GPT-Red and heat pumps rise in the US
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Meet GPT-Red: an LLM super-hacker OpenAI built to make its models safer OpenAI has built an LLM super-hacker called GPT-Red that it uses as a sparring

item.title: The agent evaluation gap: Enterprise AI organizations have a reality-alignment problem, not a coverage problem — and most are shipping to production anyway
item.description: Across 157 enterprises, organizations are granting AI agents more autonomy while trusting the evaluations meant to gate that autonomy less. Half have already shipped an agent that passed their internal evaluations and then failed a customer in production; only one in twenty fully trusts automated ev

item.title: xAI can’t deny Grok makes CSAM anymore. So it’s suing users.
item.description: Elon Musk's xAI files first lawsuit against Grok user accused of making child sex images.

item.title: Sailing the stormy seas of AI
item.description: The news landed like a thunderclap in a San Francisco conference room on June 30, 2026. Anthropic, the artificial intelligence company behind the cutting-edge Claude model, announced it will launch its own preclinical drug-discovery programs targeting neglect…

item.title: From experiment to insight: how dotmatics luma and databricks make ai-ready science a reality
item.description: * Getting the data foundation right (continuous capture, harmonization, and governance) is what actually unlocks trustworthy AI in science; AI trained on fragmented data can't be trusted. * Luma and Databricks are complementary by design: Luma brings scienti…

item.title: Anthropic is launching its own drug discovery programs for rare diseases using claude superintelligence
item.description: The news landed like a thunderclap in a San Francisco conference room on June 30, 2026. Anthropic, the artificial intelligence company behind the cutting-edge Claude model, announced it will launch its own preclinical drug-discovery programs targeting neglect…

item.title: Dynamics-enhanced molecular property prediction guided by deep learning
item.description: Author summary Predicting molecular properties is a central problem in computational biology, drug discovery and chemical safety evaluation. Molecules are dynamic objects in solution and constantly undergo conformational changes; however, most existing method…

item.title: Wiz regulates clustered protocadherin genes by restricting ctcf/cohesin loop extrusion in a genomic-distance biased manner
item.description: Author summary If you uncoiled the DNA inside a single human cell and stretched it out end-to-end, it would measure about two meters (six feet) long. Yet, this entire blueprint must be packed into the cell’s nucleus, which is microscopic—less than one-tenth t…

item.title: Contrasting evolutionary patterns of helper and sensor nrc nlrs in lettuce reflect functional divergence following subfunctionalization
item.description: Author summary Plant immune systems rely on intracellular receptors that detect pathogen molecules and activate defense responses. In many flowering plants, these receptors operate within signaling networks where “sensor” receptors detect pathogens and depend…

item.title: The global compliance challenge for multinational organizations
item.description: Change is never straightforward. It isn’t predictable. And artificial intelligence is no exception. The first few years of the generative AI era have had a Wild West quality to them. In 2026, that time of rapid, haphazard adoption has transitioned into one de…

item.title: Here’s why anthropic is pushing states to regulate AI faster
item.description: The company endorsed landmark AI transparency laws in California and New York last year, but its head of US state and local policy says they may already be outdated.

item.title: Big tech’s top executives warn enterprises are giving away too much to AI labs
item.description: Microsoft's Satya Nadella and Palantir's Alex Karp are both warning enterprises that AI labs are quietly mining the data that makes them valuable.

item.title: Show hn: rackp – a protocol for deriving fault when AI agents cause incidents
item.description: RACK Protocol — a verifiable liability assessment protocol for AI incidents. Spec, JSON schemas, and simulations (RFC-0001/0002). - rackp-io/rackp

item.title: Mhra delivers record performance as 2025-26 annual report and accounts confirms growing global influence
item.description: The Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) has published its 2025–26 Annual Report and Accounts highlighting a year of strong and consistent delivery as set out in the agency’s first Results and Forecast report earlier this year.

item.title: The other AI superpower: how russia and china are building an alternative to silicon valley
item.description: From healthcare to education, a new model of technological development aims to put everyday users – not monopolies – first Read Full Article at RT.com

item.title: Tempus highlights landmark publication demonstrating safe, real-world deployment of paige prostate AI suite in the UK national health service
item.description: Tempus Highlights Landmark Publication Demonstrating Safe, Real-World Deployment of Paige Prostate AI Suite in the UK National Health Servicebiospace.com

item.title: The g-force in the genome: unknowns on the functional flairs of dna g-quadruplexes
item.description: DNA G-quadruplexes are dynamic four-stranded DNA knots that may control genes, protect genomes, and drive disease, yet scientists still do not fully understand when they form, how they work, and why cells need them. This Unsolved Mystery investigates these qu…

item.title: Chai launches AI adoption support for public health agencies
item.description: As public health agencies fall behind in AI adoption, CHAI has launched the PULSE program to bring public health practitioners together in AI workgroups and develop playbooks.

item.title: New content from advances in methods and practices in psychological science
item.description: New content covering data, artificial intelligence, data models, and more.
